# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder_30b)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.185.0/http/server.ts";

type FilterCriteria = Record<string, string | number | boolean>;

interface User {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  email: string;
  age: number;
  active: boolean;
  createdAt: Date;
}

const kv = await Deno.openKv();

serve(async (req: Request) => {
  const url = new URL(req.url);
  const path = url.pathname;
  const method = req.method;

  if (method === "GET" && path.startsWith("/users")) {
    return await handleGetUsers(req, url);
  }

  if (method === "POST" && path === "/users") {
    return await handleCreateUser(req);
  }

  if (method === "GET" && path.startsWith("/users/")) {
    const userId = path.split("/")[3];
    return await handleGetUser(userId);
  }

  return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "Not found" }), {
    status: 404,
    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
  });
});

async function handleGetUsers(req: Request, url: URL): Promise<Response> {
  try {
    const params = url.searchParams;
    const filters: FilterCriteria = {};

    for (const [key, value] of params.entries()) {
      if (key !== "page" && key !== "limit") {
        filters[key] = value;
      }
    }

    const page = parseInt(params.get("page") || "1");
    const limit = parseInt(params.get("limit") || "10");
    const offset = (page - 1) * limit;

    const users: User[] = [];
    const iterator = kv.list<User>({ prefix: ["users"] });

    for await (const result of iterator) {
      const user = result.value;
      let matches = true;

      for (const [filterKey, filterValue] of Object.entries(filters)) {
        if (user[filterKey as keyof User] !== filterValue) {
          matches = false;
          break;
        }
      }

      if (matches) {
        users.push(user);
      }
    }

    const paginatedUsers = users.slice(offset, offset + limit);

    return new Response(JSON.stringify({
      users: paginatedUsers,
      total: users.length,
      page,
      limit
    }), {
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  } catch (error) {
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: error.message }), {
      status: 500,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  }
}

async function handleCreateUser(req: Request): Promise<Response> {
  try {
    const body = await req.json();
    const userId = crypto.randomUUID();
    const user: User = {
      id: userId,
      name: body.name,
      email: body.email,
      age: body.age,
      active: body.active ?? true,
      createdAt: new Date()
    };

    await kv.set(["users", userId], user);

    // Create dynamic keys for efficient search
    await kv.set(["users", "byEmail", user.email], userId);
    await kv.set(["users", "byName", user.name], userId);
    await kv.set(["users", "byAge", user.age.toString()], userId);
    await kv.set(["users", "byActive", user.active.toString()], userId);

    return new Response(JSON.stringify(user), {
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  } catch (error) {
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: error.message }), {
      status: 500,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  }
}

async function handleGetUser(userId: string): Promise<Response> {
  try {
    const result = await kv.get<User>(["users", userId]);
    
    if (!result.value) {
      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: "User not found" }), {
        status: 404,
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
      });
    }

    return new Response(JSON.stringify(result.value), {
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  } catch (error) {
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: error.message }), {
      status: 500,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    });
  }
}
